title: Zephyranthes phycelloides
taxonomy: Zephyranthes phycelloides belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis or lily family. Zephyranthes phycelloides is further classified under the genus Zephyranthes, which includes around 65 species of flowering plants. The genus Zephyranthes is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. These plants are characterized by their showy, funnel-shaped flowers and strap-shaped leaves.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. intro: Zephyranthes phycelloides, also known as the Yellow Zephyr Lily, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes phycelloides include alkaloids, particularly zephyranthine and phycelloidine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions or for their potential therapeutic benefits.
